title: eryngium glaciale
intro: Eryngium glaciale, also known as Alpine Sea Holly, is a perennial plant that belongs to the carrot family (Apiaceae) and is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's health benefits include relieving digestive issues, such as diarrhea and constipation, and also exhibits antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. Eryngium glaciale can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusions, and its leaves and roots are used in traditional medicine, although its use should be guided by a healthcare professional.
